notes:
cho nên means “so” or “therefore.” It links a cause to a result.

It’s a conjunction used to connect two clauses: reason first, result after. The part before explains why; the part after shows what happened because of it.

It’s similar to vì vậy or do đó, but cho nên sounds more natural in everyday speech. Don’t use it alone at the start of a sentence without a clear reason before it.

translation.en: I don't go because of the rain.

exampleSentences: Trời hôm nay mưa to, cho nên anh không đi làm được.
Chị cảm thấy mệt, cho nên chị muốn nghỉ ngơi ở nhà.
Em học chăm chỉ, cho nên em đã đạt điểm cao trong kỳ thi.
exampleSentencesTranslated: It is raining heavily today, so I can't go to work.
I feel tired, so I want to rest at home.
I studied hard, so I got a high score in the exam.

example.en: It was raining heavily, so I had to stay at home.
example.vi: Trời mưa lớn, cho nên tôi phải ở nhà.

definitions.vi: Từ dùng để chỉ nguyên nhân dẫn đến kết quả, tương tự như 'vì vậy' hoặc 'do đó'.
definitions.en: A conjunction used to indicate that one thing is the result of another; similar to 'so' or 'therefore'.

translation.en: She didn't come because she is busy.

notes_md:
**cho nên** means “so” or “therefore.” It links a cause to a result.

It’s a conjunction used to connect two clauses: reason first, result after. The part before explains why; the part after shows what happened because of it.


It’s similar to **vì vậy** or **do đó**, but *cho nên* sounds more natural in everyday speech. Don’t use it alone at the start of a sentence without a clear reason before it.
